title: My Best Friend's Success

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>My best friend from art school got a solo show at a major gallery. <break time="350ms"/>I cried for three days. <break time="300ms"/>Not happy tears.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>We graduated together. <break time="300ms"/>Struggled together. <break time="250ms"/>Applied to the same grants, the same residencies. <break time="400ms"/>And then she got her break, and I didn't. <break time="500ms"/>I was ashamed of how I felt. <break time="400ms"/>She was my friend. <break time="350ms"/>I should have been happy. <break time="300ms"/>Part of me was. <break time="250ms"/>But another part was bitter and jealous and small. <break time="500ms"/>I avoided her for months. <break time="400ms"/>Made excuses not to see her show. <break time="350ms"/>Told myself I was busy.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>Eventually I told her. <break time="400ms"/>Confessed the jealousy, the avoidance. <break time="350ms"/>She said: <break time="300ms"/>'I'd feel the same way.' <break time="500ms"/>Jealousy doesn't make you a bad person. <break time="400ms"/>It makes you human. <break time="350ms"/>What you do with it is what matters. <break time="300ms"/>I chose honesty over distance. <break time="500ms"/>We're still friends. <break time="400ms"/>And her success didn't take anything from me. <break time="350ms"/>It just felt like it did.

when_to_share: When someone is struggling with jealousy over another creative's success
